actionUnits.condition: Clock MonoBehaviour (id 314576056) from script guid 27b31662383b39d4a9c98eb14ed39152 (fileID 11500000). Let Update run for a short duration while finished==false so time accumulates and TimeDisplay() updates clockText (fileID 314576054), then manually finish to invoke BestTime.checkTime(time).
Invoke timeout path on the same Clock MonoBehaviour (id 314576056) to ensure finished flag is set via onTimeOut() even if already finished.

MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID 515040909) on Floor maps to script IfPuzzle (guid a4fe0afa74eee4b4d9b3c22a64e1dc6b). IfPuzzle subscribes to XRSocketInteractor.selectEntered to run ObjectSnapped. To test this, an IXRSelectInteractable must be snapped into Floor’s XRSocketInteractor. If the snapped object's transform.name equals correctPiece.name, IfPuzzle calls linkedIfManager.correctPiece(). In the provided scene meta, correctPiece and linkedIfManager are unassigned (fileID 0), so this path will currently produce a NullReferenceException. Please provide/assign a valid correctPiece Transform and a valid ifManager instance so we can complete this test.

MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID 515040909) on Floor maps to script IfPuzzle (guid a4fe0afa74eee4b4d9b3c22a64e1dc6b). IfPuzzle subscribes to XRSocketInteractor.selectExited to run ObjectRemoved. To test this, remove the snapped IXRSelectInteractable from Floor’s XRSocketInteractor. This calls linkedIfManager.removedPiece(). In the provided scene meta, linkedIfManager is unassigned (fileID 0), so this path will currently produce a NullReferenceException. Please provide/assign a valid ifManager instance so we can complete this test.

This Trigger simulates removing the currently selected interactable from the XR socket to fire selectExited. Verification of components: MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 1389924503) is XRSocketInteractor (m_Script {fileID:11500000, guid:60523e0647cc6ab43acc73cb266a32a7,type:3}) with m_SelectEntered/m_SelectExited UnityEvent persistent calls configured. m_SelectEntered calls GameObject(fileID:771622881).SetActive(false). m_SelectExited calls GameObject(fileID:771622881).SetActive(true). It also has m_StartingSelectedInteractable {fileID:1031835462}, so selectEntered will be triggered automatically on enable, satisfying redundancy policy (no manual trigger for entry). MonoBehaviour_1 (id: 1389924508) matches finalPuzzleSockets (m_Script {fileID:11500000, guid:f85184c8ae0d47b4c853fe653d267e42,type:3}) with linkedPuzzleManager {fileID:1293205246} and secondPartCorrect {fileID:2065480344}. On enable, it subscribes to socket.selectEntered/ObjectSnapped and socket.selectExited/ObjectRemoved. This action forces a selectExited event path to execute: XRSocketInteractor.m_SelectExited persistent call (SetActive true) and finalPuzzleSockets.ObjectRemoved(), which calls linkedPuzzleManager.decrementFirstPart() and, if the removed interactable's name equals secondPartCorrect.name, linkedPuzzleManager.decrementSecondPart(). Collider present (SphereCollider id:1389924504, isTrigger:true) allows Trigger action per rules. No direct method calls are made because XR events and listeners handle behavior, and no public parameterless methods are exposed.
